aiRating.enSummary: In the fourth quarter of 2025, MARA reported a net loss of $1.7 billion (compared to a net profit of $528 million in the same period last year), primarily due to a $1.5 billion write-down of the fair value of Bitcoin holdings caused by a roughly 30% drop in BTC prices during the quarter; revenue decreased by 6% year-over-year to $202.3 million, and adjusted EBITDA was negative.

news.text: MARA reported a $1.7B net loss in Q4 2025, compared with $528M net income a year earlier, largely due to a $1.5B markdown in the fair value of its bitcoin holdings as BTC fell about 30% during the quarter. Revenue declined 6% YoY to $202.3M, and adjusted EBITDA was negative

aiRating.enSummary: In the fourth quarter of 2025, MARA reported a net loss of $1.7 billion (compared to a net profit of $528 million in the same period last year) due to a $1.5 billion impairment loss on its Bitcoin holdings (BTC fell by approximately 30% during the quarter). Revenue decreased by 6% year-over-year to $202.3 million, and adjusted EBITDA was negative $1.49 billion. At the end of the year, MARA held 53,822 BTC, of ​​which approximately 28% were lent out or pledged.
aiRating.summary: 2025年第四季度，MARA因比特币持仓公允价值减值15亿美元（BTC当季下跌约30%），净亏损17亿美元（去年同期净利润5.28亿美元），营收同比降6%至2.023亿美元，调整后EBITDA为负14.9亿美元，年末持有53822枚BTC，约28%被借出或质押

news.text: MARA reported a $1.7B net loss in Q4 2025, compared with $528M net income a year earlier, largely due to a $1.5B markdown in the fair value of its bitcoin holdings as BTC fell about 30% during the quarter. Revenue declined 6% YoY to $202.3M, and adjusted EBITDA was negative $1.49B. MARA ended the year holding 53,822 BTC, with about 28% loaned or pledged. https://d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net/_f70cb3f4bead2071cade84a538dbeb76/marathondh/db/441/7568/file/Q4%2725+Shareholder+Letter.pdf

aiRating.enSummary: Crypto news in the past 24 hours covered a wide range of topics: Regulatoryly, Indiana's bill was approved by both houses of Congress; Hong Kong issued its first batch of fiat-backed stablecoin licenses in March; the UK's FCA selected four companies to test stablecoins; In the corporate arena, Circle's Q4 revenue increased by 77% and Tether invested in Whop; In the market, GD Culture plans to sell 7,500 BTC, and ETHZilla reduced its ETH holdings, among other things.
aiRating.summary: 过去24小时加密新闻涵盖多方面：监管上印州法案获两院批准、香港3月发首批法币稳定币牌照、英国FCA选4家测试稳定币；企业上Circle Q4营收增77%、Tether投资Whop；市场上GD Culture拟售7500 BTC、ETHZilla减持ETH等。

news.text:
Top Crypto Stories of PAST 24 Hours - @MKTNews_Global
Regulatory and Legal Developments
- Indiana’s HB 1042 received bicameral approval, aiming to open crypto investment options for public retirement plans and protect individual digital asset activities.
- Hong Kong will issue its first batch of fiat-referenced stablecoin licenses in March and plans a bill to license digital asset dealers and custodians, according to Financial Secretary Paul Chan.
- UK FCA selected Revolut, Monee Financial Technologies, ReStabilise, and VVTX to test stablecoins in real-world conditions with safeguards, observing payments, settlement, and trading capabilities.
- A ruling party lawmaker in an unspecified country proposed mandatory asset disclosures for influencers providing crypto or other investment advice, with penalties aligned to capital market sanctions.
Corporate and Strategic Moves
- World Liberty Financial (WLFI) introduced a governance proposal requiring staking of unlocked WLFI tokens for voting rights; stakers voting at least twice during the lock period earn ~2% annualized rewards from the treasury.
- Anchorage Digital CEO Nathan McCauley announced the firm holds Strategy’s STRC on its balance sheet; last month Anchorage received a $100 million equity investment from Tether.
- Stripe co-founder John Collison predicts a surge of AI agent-driven commerce using stablecoins and high-throughput blockchains; Stripe integrated USDC payments for AI agents via the x402 standard on Base.
- Project 0 launched a new Pay feature allowing users to pay credit cards by borrowing USDC against crypto portfolios.
- Safe{Labs} introduces euro-denominated yield via a Morpho vault for Société Générale’s EUR CoinVertible stablecoin, curated by DeFi advisory firm Steakhouse.
Stablecoins and Payments
- Circle reported Q4 revenue and reserve income of $770 million (up 77% YoY) as USDC in circulation rose to $75.3 billion; multi-year guidance targets a 40% CAGR for USDC.
- Circle shares surged ~30% post-earnings due to stronger-than-expected margins and rising USDC on-platform mix.
- Tether Investments disclosed a strategic stake in Whop, enabling USDT/USAT payments for 18.4M users and $3B in annual payouts across 144 countries.
- Better Home & Finance / Framework Ventures partnership provided up to $500 million in credit via the Sky stablecoin ecosystem.
- Hong Kong confirmed issuing first fiat-stablecoin licenses in March.
Bitcoin and Crypto Market Moves
- GD Culture Group (Nasdaq: GDC) board approved selling its 7,500 BTC reserve (~$510M), more than double its $210M market cap.
- Bitfinex hack LEO token trading at ~60% premium to implied fair value, with ~94,636 BTC (~30% of U.S. Strategic Bitcoin Reserve) frozen pending legal resolution.
- ETHZilla reduced ETH holdings via multiple sales ($40M in Oct, $74.5M in Dec) to fund buybacks and retire debt; pivoting to cash-flow-generating assets like tokenized home loans and aircraft engines.
- Hut 8 signed a $7B, 15-year AI lease and expanded its 2025 pipeline to 8.5GW as it pivots toward power infrastructure; results weighed down by $220M unrealized digital asset losses.
DeFi and Governance
- Aave Chan Initiative (ACI) founder Marc Zeller published an “audit” questioning Aave Labs’ track record ahead of a $51M DAO funding vote; tensions escalated again during governance discussions.
- TBD, co-founded by ex-dYdX team members, raised $3M seed round co-led by CMT Digital and ParaFi, focusing on Solana-based prediction markets requiring World ID for verified human voting.
- ETHZilla, WLFI, and other projects continue integrating staking and governance mechanisms for rewards and tokenized engagement.
Funding and Seed Rounds
- t54 Labs raised $5M seed from Anagram, Franklin Templeton, Ripple, building a trust layer for AI agentic economy with identity verification tools across blockchains.
- Stripe / PayPal analysts note Stripe’s $159B valuation dwarfs PayPal’s $43B market cap, making acquisition feasible; Venmo and PayPal consumer reach could strengthen the deal.
Market Insights
- Bitwise CIO Matt Hougan warns investors may underestimate crypto’s long-term impact due to anchoring bias, despite institutional tokenization expansion.
(https://mktnews.com/flashDetail.html?id=019c98ce-d3c3-7118-bd13-a82f0e6f75c7)

aiRating.enSummary: In the past 24 hours, several news items have emerged, including Indiana HB1042 receiving approval from both houses of parliament to open up crypto investment in retirement plans; Hong Kong issuing its first batch of fiat-backed stablecoin licenses in March; the UK's FCA selecting four companies to test stablecoins; Circle's Q4 revenue increasing by 77% to $770 million, with USDC circulation reaching 75.3 billion and its stock price rising by 30%; and Tether investing in Whop.

Top Crypto Stories of PAST 24 Hours - @MKTNews_Global
Regulatory and Legal Developments
- Indiana’s HB 1042 received bicameral approval, aiming to open crypto investment options for public retirement plans and protect individual digital asset activities.
- Hong Kong will issue its first batch of fiat-referenced stablecoin licenses in March and plans a bill to license digital asset dealers and custodians, according to Financial Secretary Paul Chan.

- TBD, co-founded by ex-dYdX team members, raised $3M seed round co-led by CMT Digital and ParaFi, focusing on Solana-based prediction markets requiring World ID for verified human voting.

aiRating.enSummary: Crypto news in the past 24 hours: Binance denies firing investigators over Iranian funding flows; Terraform Labs sues Jane Street for alleged insider trading; Chainlink's Taylor Lindman joins the SEC's crypto task force; several institutions have made moves regarding mining, financing, and compliance.
aiRating.summary: 过去24小时加密领域新闻：Binance否认因伊朗资金流解雇调查人员；Terraform Labs起诉Jane Street涉嫌内幕交易；Chainlink的Taylor Lindman加入SEC加密工作组；多家机构在挖矿、融资、合规等方面有动态。

news.text:
Top Crypto Stories of PAST 24 Hours - @MKTNews_Global
Regulatory and Legal Developments
- Binance denies firing investigators over reported $1.7 billion crypto flows to Iran.
- Terraform Labs sues Jane Street for alleged insider trading prior to the Terra-Luna collapse (WSJ).
- Chainlink’s Taylor Lindman joins SEC as chief counsel for the crypto task force.
- Australian police charge a man over his role in an alleged $3.5 million crypto investment scam targeting elderly victims.
Mining and Infrastructure
- Canaan acquires Cipher Mining’s stake in West Texas bitcoin mining projects in a $40 million deal.
- Bitdeer CEO says bitcoin balance “will not always be zero” as the miner eyes land acquisitions after liquidating its entire treasury.
- Pantera-backed Solana Company begins APAC staking infrastructure buildout.
Institutional and Market Commentary
- Bitwise CIO Matt Hougan says we’re in a classic crypto winter, but fundamentals are racing ahead of price and expects a 2027 recovery.
- Global crypto ETP outflows stretch to the fifth week with $4 billion exiting funds amid growing investor apathy (CoinShares).
- Stablecoins could drive $1 trillion in T-bill demand, giving Treasury room to shift issuance (Standard Chartered).
- Missouri advances bitcoin reserve bill to House committee, reviving the crypto treasury push.
Corporate and Funding News
- Aave faces $50 million funding controversy with ACI Founder Marc Zeller.
- Hyperliquid Web3 “SuperApp” Based raises $11.5 million Series A led by Pantera.
- Backpack to offer users access to equity representing 20% of the company via one-year token staking.
- Crypto. com secures conditional OCC approval in bid to become a federally regulated bank.
Bitcoin Investment Moves
- Michael Saylor’s Strategy buys another 592 bitcoin for $40 million: “If it’s not going to zero, it’s going to a million.”
Stablecoin Initiatives
- Trump’s Board of Peace explores a stablecoin to support digital transactions in Gaza (FT).
- Trump-backed World Liberty Financial says a USD1 short and social media attack failed as its stablecoin briefly dipped.
Ethereum / DeFi Ecosystem
- Ethereum Foundation forms a team to support protocol development, stating it “believes in Defipunk.”
- South Korea’s Hanwha taps Jito Foundation for liquidity staking ETPs. (https://mktnews.com/flashDetail.html?id=019c8e70-f483-7118-bcf5-cc0047def732)

aiRating.enSummary: Bitdeer stated on its X platform that its decision to sell Bitcoin is not a cause for market concern. It is currently evaluating several opportunities to acquire power and land assets without binding restrictions, believing that preparing liquidity in advance is a prudent move. It emphasized that the hash rate will continue to grow and that it will continue to mine more Bitcoin for the benefit of shareholders.
aiRating.summary: Bitdeer在X平台表示其出售比特币的决定无需市场担忧，目前正评估多项非约束性收购电力和土地资产的机会，认为提前准备流动性是审慎之举，强调哈希率将持续增长并为股东利益继续挖矿更多比特币。

news.text: Bitdeer stated on X that its decision to sell Bitcoin should not be a cause for market concern. The company said it is currently evaluating several non-binding opportunities to acquire power and land assets and believes it is prudent to prepare liquidity in advance. Bitdeer emphasized that its hashrate will continue to grow and that it will keep mining more Bitcoin in the interest of shareholders. https://x.com/BitdeerOfficial/status/2025839516809093324

aiRating.summary: 1月全球加密货币挖矿新闻：挖矿盈利能力承压，MARA持有超52k BTC（全球第二），Bitmain降价矿机，北美矿池份额降至35%，多国调整挖矿政策，企业财库6个月净增持260k BTC超新增供应。

news.text: Global Crypto Mining News in January: Mining profitability under pressure. MARA transferred >2,125 BTC but held >52k BTC (second globally). Rigs like S19 XP+ Hyd neared shutdown; S21 at $69K–$74K. Bitmain cut S19/S21 prices to $3–$8/TH/s, expanded hosting bundles. North American pools fell to 35% share amid AI/HPC pivot. Bitdeer self-mined 636 BTC (Dec 2025, 55.2 EH/s); CleanSpark 7,746 BTC (2025); Bit Digital exited BTC for ETH/AI. Regional: Ethiopia seeks partners; Russia tightens illegal mining rules; Malaysia raids illegals; Turkmenistan legalizes mining/exchanges (Jan 1). Corporate treasuries net +260k BTC in 6 months, exceeding new supply. Read more https://wublock.substack.com/p/global-crypto-mining-news-in-january?r=jbpop&utm_campaign=post&utm_medium=web&triedRedirect=true

news.text:
Crypto stocks pare gains as bitcoin retreats from $90,000 rally.
Crypto-linked stocks pulled back, with miners like MARA Holdings (MARA) down 4.8% and Core Scientific (CORZ) down 6%.
